sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ['B', 'W', 'c', 'G']:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))
        if center[0] > 5:
            print(f"Node: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({center[0]:.0f},{center[1]:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blob shrunk from 69 to 60 pixels (lost 9 pixels), and the purple block is at (30, 33) with 9 pixels. The blob center moved from (27, 33) to (28, 33).
So when the purple block got closer to the blob, the blob shrunk by 9 pixels (the block's size). This confirms that blocks close to the blob cause it to shrink.
The blob went from 69 -> 65 -> 69 -> 60 pixels. It seems like:
- When a block is close to the blob, the blob shrinks by the block's size
- When a block touches/overlaps the blob, the blob grows by the block's size
The goal might be to shrink the blob to 0 pixels by keeping blocks close to it without touching it.
But wait, the block at (30, 33) is very close to the blob at (28, 33). The blob shrunk from 69 to 60. If I keep moving this block closer, will the blob continue to shrink?
Let me move the block even closer to the blob to see if it shrinks further.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move purple block at (30, 33) closer to blob at (28, 33): click at (29, 33)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 29, 'col': 33}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
